title: Microcos calymmatosepala
intro: Microcos calymmatosepala, commonly known as the 'Bentong ginger', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the relief of pain and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Microcos calymmatosepala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and capsules, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal supplements and remedies.
taxonomy: Microcos calymmatosepala is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Borneo. The classification of Microcos calymmatosepala is further broken down into its genus Microcos and its species calymmatosepala. The genus Microcos is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the "touch-me-nots" due to their sensitive leaves. The classification of Microcos calymmatosepala is a part of the larger classification system for the plant kingdom.
